name: Kue
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans-serif with variable contrast and mixed terminals; single-sided rounding and conceptual construction place it firmly in the geometric tradition.
summary: A variable geometric sans by Ondrej Jób with single-sided rounding and adjustable optical contrast across two styles.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób and released under Setup Type, Kue is a conceptual geometric sans-serif built around mixed terminals and variable contrast, with a medium x-height suited to display settings. Two styles carry variable axes for contrast, width and optical weight, supported by three stylistic sets that include rounded alternates and short-slash variants. Coverage spans Basic Latin and Extended Latin across more than 100 languages with 428 glyphs total.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Kue as a conceptual variable geometric sans-serif with mixed terminals, variable contrast, natural width and medium x-height, primarily intended for display use. It ships in 2 styles, covers over 100 languages via Basic Latin and Extended Latin, and offers 428 glyphs with proportional lining figures and stylistic sets 1 through 3 for further customisation.

name: Syntact
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric, industrial monospaced sans-serif with no contrast, narrow width, and angled terminals. The monospaced nature and geometric construction place it firmly in the geometric sans category with monospaced characteristics.
summary: A geometric, industrial monospaced sans in five weights, built with no contrast, narrow proportions and angled terminals across 627 glyphs.
description: Syntact is a narrow, monospaced geometric sans with a brutalist sensibility, offering five styles from Thin to Bold with no stroke contrast and distinctive angled terminals. The family covers Basic Latin and Extended Latin across more than 100 languages, and includes tabular lining figures alongside superscript, subscript and fraction variants for technical and editorial setting. Five stylistic sets allow further customisation, including single-storey alternates and a variant y.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Syntact as a geometric, industrial monospaced sans-serif with angled terminals intended for versatile use. It spans 5 styles from Thin to Bold with narrow width and medium x-height, supports over 100 languages via Basic and Extended Latin, and features 627 glyphs including tabular lining figures, superscript, subscript, fractions and stylistic sets 1–5.

name: Perti
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes Perti as an industrial, rounded slab-serif with no stroke contrast and rounded terminals, placing it firmly in the slab-serif category with a rounded subcategory.
summary: A rounded slab-serif with no stroke contrast, six weights and uni-width proportions, designed by Ondrej Jób for versatile industrial use.
description: Six weights from Thin to Heavy share consistent character widths across the range, a feature the foundry labels uni-width non-monospace. The design has no stroke contrast and rounded terminals throughout, giving it a firm, mechanical quality without the rigidity of a purely geometric slab. A stylistic set swaps to single-storey a and g, and the character set covers over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin with proportional and tabular lining figures included.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Perti as a classic, full-bodied, industrial, rounded slab-serif with rounded terminals, no contrast, natural width and a medium x-height. It comprises six styles from Thin to Heavy, covers over 100 languages, and includes proportional lining figures, tabular lining figures, fractions, stylistic set 1 and case-sensitive forms across 443 glyphs.

name: Kinki
classification_notes: Described explicitly as an organic handwritten typeface with rounded terminals and variable contrast, intended primarily for display use; fits squarely within the script/handwritten category.
summary: A single-weight organic handwritten display face by Ondrej Jób, built with rounded terminals, variable contrast and a large x-height.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób and released in 2025, Kinki is a display-oriented handwritten typeface with rounded terminals and natural, variable contrast that gives it an expressive, fluid quality. The family consists of one style with 458 glyphs covering Basic Latin and Extended Latin across more than 100 languages. Stylistic Set 1 provides seriffed alternates on certain characters, and standard ligatures round out the customisation options.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Kinki as an expressive, organic handwritten typeface with rounded terminals, primarily intended for display use. It consists of one style with variable contrast, natural width and a large x-height, covering over 100 languages with 458 glyphs. Additional features include Stylistic Set 1 and standard ligatures for further customisation.

name: Bauplan
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a geometric sans-serif guided by an interpretation of Futura, with low contrast, natural width and medium x-height — hallmarks of the geometric sans tradition.
summary: A geometric sans-serif in 14 styles, guided by Futura but redrawn with Setup Type's emphasis on rationality and everyday usability.
description: Spanning Thin to Black with matching italics, Bauplan keeps low contrast, natural widths and a medium x-height throughout. Two stylistic sets offer alternate forms including a round u and seriffed I and J, extending its range from neutral body text to more characterful display use. Coverage runs to over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin, with 457 glyphs including proportional lining figures, superscript, subscript and fractions.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Bauplan as a classic, modern geometric sans-serif designed for versatile use, framed as an interpretation of Futura guided by their approach to rationality and usability. The family ships in 14 styles from Thin to Black with italics, supports over 100 languages, and includes advanced numerical features alongside stylistic sets for further customisation.

name: Hauer Display
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes Hauer Display as a transitional serif with high contrast and natural width, placing it squarely in the transitional category between old-style and modern serifs.
summary: A high-contrast transitional serif from Ondrej Jób spanning eight weights from Hair to Black, designed expressly for display setting.
description: Eight weights from Hair to Black give Hauer Display a wide tonal range suited to headlines and large-scale typesetting. High contrast strokes, natural width and a medium x-height give it a classical disposition, while three stylistic sets offer conservative, single-storey and flared alternates for further variation. Coverage extends across Basic Latin and Extended Latin for over 100 languages, with proportional oldstyle and tabular lining figures alongside fractions for numerical work.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Hauer Display as an elegant, refined transitional serif intended primarily for display use, with 8 styles from Hair to Black, high contrast, natural width and medium x-height. It supports Basic Latin and Extended Latin across 100-plus languages, with 535 glyphs including proportional lining and oldstyle figures, tabular lining figures, fractions and slashed zero, plus three stylistic sets for customisation.

name: Mogul
classification_notes: The foundry describes Mogul explicitly as a humanist sans-serif with low contrast and large x-height, drawing direct inspiration from Universal Grotesk while maintaining natural proportions.
summary: A humanist sans-serif by Ondrej Jób, offered in eight weights from Hair to Black and described as a love-letter to Universal Grotesk.
description: Drawing on the proportions and spirit of Universal Grotesk, Mogul is built around low contrast, a large x-height and natural width across eight styles. The family covers over 100 languages via Basic Latin and Extended Latin, and includes proportional and tabular lining figures, superscript, subscript, fractions and a slashed zero. Three stylistic sets, case-sensitive forms and ordinals offer further typographic control.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Mogul as a full-bodied, modern humanist sans-serif designed for versatile use, spanning eight styles from Hair to Black with low contrast, natural width and a large x-height. It supports over 100 languages across Basic Latin and Extended Latin, and includes 587 glyphs with an extensive range of figure styles and OpenType features including stylistic sets, case-sensitive forms and ordinals.

name: Async Sans
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a neo-grotesk by the foundry, with low contrast, natural width and medium x-height — hallmarks of the neo-grotesque genre refined through precise, utilitarian construction.
summary: A tightly constructed neo-grotesque from Ondrej Jób, spanning nine weights from Hair to Ultra with low contrast and a suite of OpenType stylistic alternates.
description: Async Sans runs nine styles from Hair to Ultra, built around low contrast, natural width and a medium x-height suited to both text and display use. Eight stylistic sets cover alternates including single-storey 'a', two-storey 'g', spurless forms and perpendicular terminals, giving designers meaningful control over character. Coverage extends to Basic Latin, Extended Latin and Greek across 101 languages, with proportional and tabular lining figures included.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Async Sans as a precision-machined neo-grotesk intended for versatile use. The family comprises nine styles and 684 glyphs, supporting over 101 languages across Basic Latin, Extended Latin and Greek. Features include stylistic sets 1–8, case-sensitive forms, proportional and tabular lining figures, and a slashed zero.

name: Preon
classification_notes: Self-described as a 'humanoid sans' with mixed terminals and low contrast; the combination of industrial character with organic terminal variation places it within the humanist sans tradition, though with an experimental edge.
summary: An experimental, wide-set sans-serif by Ondrej Jób with mixed terminals, low contrast and a large x-height across 14 styles from Thin to Black.
description: Described by Setup Type as a 'humanoid sans', Preon blends industrial character with organic mixed terminals across seven weights with matching italics. Its wide proportions and large x-height make it well-suited to both display and text settings. Four stylistic sets offer alternate letterforms including a simplified t, double-storey a, spurred r and u, and a stencil variant, alongside a full suite of numeral styles and over 100 language scripts.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Preon as an experimental, industrial hybrid sans-serif with mixed terminals designed for versatile use. It consists of 14 styles from Thin to Black with italics, featuring low contrast, wide width and a large x-height. The family supports Basic Latin and Extended Latin across more than 100 languages, with 634 glyphs including proportional lining figures, superscript, subscript, fractions and stylistic sets 1 through 4.

name: Preon Mix
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as an experimental, industrial hybrid sans-serif with mixed terminals, low contrast and wide proportions — consistent with an industrial or utilitarian sans subcategory rather than a classical grotesque or humanist.
summary: An industrial hybrid sans-serif from Ondrej Jób with mixed terminals, wide proportions and a large x-height across 14 styles from Thin to Black.
description: Wide-set and low in contrast, Preon Mix pairs mixed terminals with a large x-height to produce a utilitarian character suited to display settings. Seven weights run from Thin to Black, each with a matching italic, and four stylistic sets offer alternates including a double-storey 'a', simplified 't', spurred 'r' and 'u', and a stencil 'o'. Coverage spans Basic and Extended Latin across more than 100 languages, with proportional lining figures supplemented by superscript, subscript and fractional forms.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Preon Mix as an experimental, industrial hybrid sans-serif typeface with mixed terminals, primarily intended for display use. It comprises 14 styles from Thin to Black including italics, featuring low contrast, wide width and a large x-height. The family supports Basic and Extended Latin covering over 100 languages, with 634 glyphs and OpenType features including four stylistic sets, case-sensitive forms, ordinals and a range of figure styles.

name: Preon Hard
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as an industrial geometric sans with low contrast, wide proportions and a large x-height; the modular, stencil-adjacent construction and display orientation align firmly with geometric sans classification.
summary: A wide, low-contrast geometric sans from Ondrej Jób built for display use across 14 styles from Thin to Black with italics.
description: Preon Hard runs seven weights with matching italics, all cut wide with a large x-height and low stroke contrast that gives it an industrial, machine-like character. Four stylistic sets offer alternates including a double-storey 'a', simplified 't', spurred 'r' and 'u', and open stencil forms, broadening its range for editorial and branding work. Coverage spans over 100 languages across Basic Latin and Extended Latin, with a full complement of figure styles and OpenType features for numerical setting.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Preon Hard as an experimental industrial geometric sans-serif primarily intended for display use, consisting of 14 styles from Thin to Black including italics, with low contrast, wide width and a large x-height. The typeface supports Basic Latin and Extended Latin across more than 100 languages, with 634 glyphs including proportional lining figures, superscript, subscript and fractions, plus stylistic sets 1–4, case-sensitive forms and ordinals.

name: Preon Soft
classification_notes: Described explicitly as an industrial geometric sans-serif with rounded terminals, wide proportions, low contrast and a large x-height, placing it firmly in the geometric sans tradition.
summary: A wide, rounded geometric sans-serif with low contrast and a large x-height, offered in fourteen styles from Thin to Black with matching italics.
description: Built on industrial geometric principles with rounded terminals, Preon Soft covers 14 styles and over 100 languages across Basic Latin and Extended Latin. The family includes four stylistic sets offering alternates such as a double-storey 'a', spurred 'r' and 'u', and a simplified 't', alongside a full suite of figure styles including superscript, subscript and fractions. Its wide width and large x-height give it a strong presence at display sizes while remaining readable in text.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Preon Soft as an experimental, industrial geometric sans-serif with rounded terminals designed for versatile use. It consists of 14 styles ranging from Thin to Black with italics, features low contrast, wide proportions and a large x-height, and supports over 100 languages with 634 glyphs including proportional lining figures, fractions and four stylistic sets.

name: Clarinet
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a modern serif with high contrast and flared terminals, drawing on multiple historical periods of letterform evolution. The combination of chiselled details and round proportions places it firmly in the modern/transitional serif tradition.
summary: A high-contrast modern serif with flared terminals and round proportions, spanning eight weights from Hair to Black.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób, Clarinet draws on multiple historical periods of letterform evolution to produce a serif with wide, round proportions, chiselled details and a medium x-height. Eight weights from Hair to Black cover over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin, with 396 glyphs including proportional oldstyle figures, tabular lining figures and standard ligatures. A stylistic set offers single-storey alternates for a and g, giving designers additional flexibility for branding, packaging and editorial work.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Clarinet as an elegant, sophisticated modern serif with flared terminals, intended for versatile use across branding, packaging and editorial design. They characterise it as an orchestration of multiple historical influences into a classical and elegant yet friendly and warm typeface, with round and wide proportions and chiselled details.

name: Clarinet Compressed
classification_notes: High contrast between thick and thin strokes with flared terminals and compressed proportions places this firmly in the modern serif tradition, drawing on neoclassical letterform conventions.
summary: A compressed modern serif by Ondrej Jób with high contrast, flared terminals and eight weights from Hair to Black.
description: Clarinet Compressed draws on multiple periods of letterform history to produce a high-contrast serif with chiselled detail and a compressed width suited to display and editorial work. Eight styles run from Hair to Black, with proportional lining figures as default alongside proportional oldstyle and tabular lining alternates. OpenType features include a stylistic set offering single-storey 'a', standard ligatures and ordinals, across a glyph set covering over 100 languages in Basic and Extended Latin.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Clarinet Compressed as an elegant, sophisticated modern serif with flared terminals, high contrast and a medium x-height, intended for versatile use in branding, packaging and editorial design. The family consists of 8 styles from Hair to Black, supports over 100 languages, and includes 396 glyphs with multiple figure styles and OpenType features such as a stylistic set, standard ligatures and ordinals.

name: Clarinet Condensed
classification_notes: High contrast between thick and thin strokes with flared terminals and narrow proportions place this squarely in the modern serif tradition, condensed in width and intended for display and editorial use.
summary: A condensed modern serif with high contrast and flared terminals, offered in eight weights from Hair to Black for display and editorial use.
description: Clarinet Condensed runs from Hair to Black across eight styles, combining the high-contrast stroke modulation of a classical modern serif with a narrow width and medium x-height. Flared terminals give the letterforms a chiselled warmth that distinguishes them from starker Didone models. The family covers Basic Latin and Extended Latin across more than 100 languages, and ships with proportional lining, proportional oldstyle and tabular lining figures alongside stylistic set, ligature and ordinal features.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Clarinet Condensed as an elegant, sophisticated modern serif with flared terminals, designed for versatile use in branding, packaging and editorial design. Eight styles from Hair to Black pair high contrast and narrow width with a medium x-height. The family supports over 100 languages across Basic Latin and Extended Latin, with 396 glyphs including multiple figure sets and OpenType features such as a stylistic set, standard ligatures and ordinals.

name: Clarinet Wide
classification_notes: High-contrast construction with flared terminals and wide proportions places this squarely in the modern serif tradition, drawing on classical letterform influences while maintaining warmth through round, open shapes.
summary: A high-contrast wide-proportion serif by Ondrej Jób, spanning eight weights from Hair to Black with flared terminals and classical detailing.
description: Built on wide proportions and a medium x-height, the family draws from multiple periods of letterform history to arrive at something that reads as both classical and approachable. Eight styles from Hair to Black provide a broad tonal range, supported by proportional oldstyle figures, tabular lining figures and a stylistic set offering single-storey alternate forms. Coverage extends to over 100 languages across Basic Latin and Extended Latin in 396 glyphs per style.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Clarinet Wide as an elegant, sophisticated modern serif with flared terminals, high contrast, wide proportions and a medium x-height. They position it as an orchestration of multiple historical influences in letterform evolution, yielding a typeface that is classical and elegant yet friendly and warm, suited to branding, packaging and editorial design.

name: Clarinet Display Condensed
classification_notes: A high-contrast modern serif with flared terminals and narrow proportions, placing it firmly in the display serif tradition akin to Didone or neoclassical models, adapted for condensed display use.
summary: A high-contrast condensed serif by Ondrej Jób, built across eight weights from Hair to Black for display-scale branding and editorial work.
description: With very high stroke contrast, flared terminals and a narrow width, Clarinet Display Condensed is designed for headlines and large-scale settings. Eight weights from Hair to Black give a wide tonal range without italics, and the family covers over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin. Proportional oldstyle and tabular lining figures sit alongside stylistic alternates and standard ligatures for refined setting.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Clarinet Display Condensed as an elegant, sophisticated modern serif with flared terminals, very high contrast, narrow width and medium x-height, intended for versatile use in branding, packaging and editorial design. The typeface draws on multiple historical periods of letterform evolution to produce a result they characterise as classical and elegant yet friendly and warm. It ships with 396 glyphs, three figure styles and supports over 100 languages.

name: Clarinet Display Compressed
classification_notes: A modern serif with very high stroke contrast, flared terminals, compressed proportions and medium x-height, placing it firmly in the high-contrast display serif tradition.
summary: A compressed, high-contrast modern serif in eight weights from Hair to Black, designed for display use in branding, packaging and editorial contexts.
description: Clarinet Display Compressed draws on multiple historical periods of letterform design to produce a classical yet warm result, characterised by very high stroke contrast, flared terminals and a medium x-height. Eight weights run from Hair to Black with no italic cut, and the family covers over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin with 396 glyphs. Proportional lining figures are the default, supplemented by proportional oldstyle and tabular lining figures, alongside stylistic alternates and standard ligatures.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Clarinet Display Compressed as an elegant, sophisticated modern serif with flared terminals intended for versatile use. They note it as an orchestration of influences from various historical periods of letterform evolution, producing a result that is classical and elegant yet friendly and warm, and recommend it for branding, packaging and editorial design.

name: Ocarina
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes it as a geometric sans-serif with a humanist touch, featuring low contrast and natural width, consistent with the geometric sans subcategory.
summary: A 16-style geometric sans with humanist warmth, spanning Hair to Black with italics and broad Latin language support across 100+ languages.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób, Ocarina pairs geometric construction with a friendly, approachable character through low contrast, natural width and a medium x-height. The family runs across eight weights from Hair to Black, each with a matching italic, totalling 16 styles. Figure support is thorough, covering proportional lining and oldstyle, tabular lining and oldstyle, superscript, subscript and fractions, with additional features including a stylistic set with alternate a, g and u forms.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Ocarina as an elegant, friendly geometric sans-serif intended for versatile use, comprising 16 styles from Hair to Black including italics. It supports Basic Latin and Extended Latin, covering over 100 languages with 603 glyphs. Additional OpenType features include stylistic set 1, case sensitive forms and a full range of figure styles for advanced numerical setting.

name: Clarinet Display
classification_notes: High-contrast vertical stress with flared terminals and narrow hairlines characteristic of modern serifs in the Didone tradition, though with wider, warmer proportions than strict Didones.
summary: A high-contrast modern serif with flared terminals and wide proportions, spanning eight weights from Hair to Black for display and branding use.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób, Clarinet Display draws on multiple historical periods of letterform design to produce something classically structured yet warm in proportion. Eight weights run from Hair to Black, each sharing the same natural width and medium x-height with very high stroke contrast. The family covers over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin, and includes proportional oldstyle and tabular lining figures alongside stylistic sets and standard ligatures.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Clarinet Display as an elegant, sophisticated modern serif with flared terminals built for versatile use across branding, packaging and editorial design. Eight styles from Hair to Black offer high contrast, natural width and medium x-height, with 396 glyphs covering over 100 languages and additional features including stylistic set 1, standard ligatures, ordinals and multiple figure styles.

name: Clarinet Display Wide
classification_notes: A modern serif with very high stroke contrast and flared terminals, wide proportions, and a classical construction typical of display-oriented modern serifs in the Didone lineage.
summary: A wide, high-contrast display serif in eight weights from Hair to Black, with flared terminals and a classical construction suited to branding and editorial work.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób, Clarinet Display Wide draws on multiple historical periods of letterform design to produce something round and wide-proportioned with chiselled details and strong stroke contrast. Eight weights run from Hair to Black, and the character set of 396 glyphs includes proportional lining, proportional oldstyle and tabular lining figures. Stylistic set 1 offers single-storey alternates for 'a' and 'g', with standard ligatures and ordinals rounding out the OpenType features.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Clarinet Display Wide as an elegant, sophisticated modern serif with flared terminals and very high contrast, available in eight styles from Hair to Black. They position it as an orchestration of multiple historical influences into a classical yet friendly design with round, wide proportions and chiselled details, suited to branding, packaging and editorial use. It supports Basic Latin and Extended Latin across more than 100 languages, with 396 glyphs and multiple figure styles.

name: Ocarina Rough
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans-serif with a humanist touch; low contrast, natural width, and medium x-height place it firmly in the geometric sans tradition with humanist softening.
summary: A geometric sans-serif with a humanist touch from Ondrej Jób, offered in 16 styles from Hair to Black with matching italics.
description: Running from Hair to Black with italics throughout, the family pairs low contrast and a medium x-height with subtle humanist warmth. A stylistic set offers alternative forms for 'a', 'g' and 'u', and the 603-glyph set covers proportional and tabular figures in both lining and oldstyle cuts, plus fractions and superscripts for careful typesetting. Latin Extended coverage reaches over 100 languages, making it practical for international work.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Ocarina Rough as an elegant, friendly geometric sans-serif built for versatile use across 16 styles, with low contrast, natural width, and medium x-height. It supports Basic Latin and Extended Latin covering over 100 languages, and includes advanced figure sets, stylistic alternates, case-sensitive forms, and subscript figures for further customisation.

name: Ocarina Soft
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans-serif with low contrast, medium x-height, and natural width; the soft, rounded character construction places it firmly in the geometric sans tradition.
summary: A softly rounded geometric sans-serif from Ondrej Jób spanning eight weights with matching italics, designed for versatile use across text and display settings.
description: Sixteen styles run from Hair to Black with full italic counterparts, all sharing low contrast, natural width and a medium x-height that keeps the family usable across a broad range of sizes. Coverage extends across Basic and Extended Latin for over 100 languages, with 603 glyphs including proportional and tabular figure sets, oldstyle figures, superscript, subscript and fractions. A stylistic set offering alternate a, g and u forms adds flexibility for those wanting a more neutral or more characterful setting.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Ocarina Soft as an elegant, friendly geometric sans-serif built for versatile use, offering 16 styles from Hair to Black with italics, low contrast, natural width and medium x-height. The family covers over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin and includes an extensive figure system alongside features such as stylistic set 1, case sensitive forms and subscript figures.

name: Setup Grotesk
classification_notes: Explicitly geometric construction with tightly closed counters, horizontal stroke terminals, and two constructional variants (pointy and flat joints), placing it squarely in the geometric sans tradition, drawing from Avant Garde and Futura lineages.
summary: A wide, low-contrast geometric sans by Ondrej Jób, offered in eight weights from Hair to Black with two constructional variants for diagonal stroke joints.
description: Built on geometric principles closer to Futura than Avant Garde, Setup Grotesk differentiates itself through higher width variability between glyphs and a choice between pointy and flat diagonal connections via stylistic sets. Eight weights from Hair to Black cover display-oriented use, while broad script support spanning Latin Extended, Vietnamese, Cyrillic and Greek extends its reach across more than 110 languages. A full suite of figure styles, small capitals and ordinals rounds out a well-specified OpenType feature set for a typeface primarily aimed at display work.
foundry_blurb: Setup Grotesk is described by the foundry as a no-nonsense geometric sans-serif primarily intended for display use, with low contrast, wide width and medium x-height. It features eight styles, two constructional variants (pointy and flat stroke connections), support for over 110 languages, and 1,431 glyphs including multiple figure sets, small capitals and six stylistic sets.

name: Setup Serif
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes it as a humanist serif with medium contrast, natural width, and medium x-height, intended primarily for text use.
summary: A text-focused humanist serif by Ondrej Jób, offered in 14 styles from Thin to Black with matching italics and comprehensive figure sets.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób, this humanist serif is built for continuous text with medium contrast, natural width and a medium x-height. Fourteen styles cover seven weights from Thin to Black, each with a corresponding italic, and the glyph set of 550 characters includes proportional oldstyle figures by default alongside lining, tabular, superscript, subscript and fraction variants. Latin Extended coverage reaches over 100 languages, with additional OpenType features including two stylistic sets, standard ligatures and case-sensitive forms.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Setup Serif as a versatile workhorse humanist serif primarily intended for text use, with 14 styles from Thin to Black including italics, medium contrast, natural width and medium x-height. It supports Basic Latin and Extended Latin across over 100 languages, and offers a full suite of figure styles alongside stylistic sets, standard ligatures and case-sensitive forms.

name: Diform
classification_notes: The foundry describes Diform explicitly as a geometric sans-serif with low contrast, natural width and medium x-height, fitting squarely within the geometric sans subcategory.
summary: A bare-bones geometric sans in 16 styles from Hair to Black, designed by Ondrej Jób for straightforward, versatile use across print and screen.
description: Sixteen styles spanning Hair to Black with matching italics give Diform a broad working range for both text and display settings. Low contrast, natural width and a medium x-height keep it practical rather than characterful. Coverage extends to over 100 languages via Basic and Extended Latin, with a full suite of figure variants and OpenType features including a stylistic alternate set and case-sensitive forms.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Diform as a bare-bones, functional geometric sans-serif intended for versatile use, comprising 16 styles from Hair to Black including italics. It features low contrast, natural width and medium x-height, supports over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin, and includes 513 glyphs with proportional and tabular lining figures, superscript, subscript, fractions, stylistic set 1 and case-sensitive forms.

name: Setup Serif Display
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly classifies it as a humanist serif; high contrast and display orientation place it firmly in the display humanist serif tradition.
summary: A high-contrast humanist serif by Ondrej Jób, offered in seven weights from Thin to Black and built primarily for display use.
description: Spanning seven weights with very high contrast and a medium x-height, this display serif is intended as a headline workhorse across a broad range of editorial and branding contexts. The default figure set uses proportional oldstyle numerals, with tabular lining, tabular oldstyle, superscript, subscript and fractions also available. Coverage extends to over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin, with stylistic sets and case-sensitive forms for additional control.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Setup Serif Display as a versatile, workhorse humanist serif typeface primarily intended for display use, consisting of 7 styles from Thin to Black with very high contrast, natural width and medium x-height, covering over 100 languages with 550 glyphs and a comprehensive suite of figure styles and OpenType features.

name: Sonda
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a neo-grotesque with rounded terminals, low contrast and medium x-height, placing it firmly in the neo-grotesque subcategory with a playful, softened character.
summary: A rounded neo-grotesque by Ondrej Jób spanning eight weights from Hair to Black, with low contrast and softened terminals suited to both text and display use.
description: Eight weights from Hair to Black give Sonda a broad range for both editorial and UI contexts, with rounded terminals and low contrast lending the family a friendly, approachable tone. Support extends across Basic Latin and Extended Latin for over 100 languages, while 521 glyphs include superscript, subscript, fractions and a slashed zero. Stylistic sets and standard and discretionary ligatures allow for customisation across different setting contexts.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Sonda as a playful neo-grotesque sans-serif with rounded terminals intended for versatile use, offered in 8 styles from Hair to Black. The family features low contrast, natural width and a medium x-height, with support for over 100 languages across Basic Latin and Extended Latin. Advanced features include proportional lining figures, superscript, subscript, fractions, stylistic sets 1–3, standard and discretionary ligatures, case-sensitive forms and ordinals.

name: Monoxil Pro
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a monospaced sans-serif with fixed character spacing, low contrast and a large x-height, making it a clear monospaced sans classification.
summary: A friendly monospaced sans-serif in 16 styles from Hair to Black, drawn from medical typography aesthetics and expanded from an earlier school project.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób and released in January 2023, Monoxil Pro is an expanded successor to the original Monoxil typeface, softening its predecessor's mechanical character into something more approachable. The family runs 16 styles across eight weights with matching italics, supported by 556 glyphs covering over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin. Proportional width stylistic variants for normally compressed or expanded characters allow smoother text setting while retaining the monospace aesthetic.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Monoxil Pro as a friendly monospaced sans-serif intended for versatile use, featuring low contrast, natural width and a large x-height. An expanded version of the original Monoxil, which was inspired by medical typography and created as a school project, the Pro version offers 16 styles, over 100 language support, tabular lining figures, fractions, three stylistic sets and proportional width variants for select characters.

name: Manual Grotesk A
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans-serif with no contrast, narrow width and a large x-height, based on a mid-20th century manually constructed model. Utilitarian geometry with socialist-era signage origins places it firmly in the geometric sans tradition.
summary: A digitisation of Richard Pípal's mid-20th century Slovak street signage model, preserved and re-introduced by Ondrej Jób in six styles.
description: Based almost without divergence on Richard Pípal's original model for a manually constructed typeface, this narrow geometric sans was practically omnipresent on Slovak street signage during the socialist era. Six styles with no stroke contrast and a large x-height make it suited to display and wayfinding contexts. Stylistic sets, small capitals and ordinals extend its utility beyond its utilitarian origins.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Manual Grotesk A as a utilitarian geometric sans-serif intended primarily for display use, built on Richard Pípal's original manually constructed typeface model from the mid-20th century. Once omnipresent on Slovak street signage plates during the socialist era, its digitisation is both a preservation effort and an attempt to re-introduce it in new contexts. The family covers over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin with 580 glyphs and features including six stylistic sets, small capitals and ordinals.

name: Manual Pozor Round
classification_notes: A caps-only oblique geometric sans-serif with no stroke contrast, derived from Slovak warning sign lettering. Its rounded terminals and narrow width place it firmly in display rather than text use.
summary: A caps-only oblique geometric sans digitised from old Slovak warning signs, with soft rounded terminals that mimic the effect of enamel production on letterforms.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób and released in 2018, Manual Pozor Round is the oblique companion to Manual Grotesk B, derived from the lettering used exclusively on Slovak warning signs rather than street markers. Seven styles run from Thin to Ultra with no stroke contrast and a narrow width, while rounded corners and filled joints reproduce the softening that occurred when letters were fired in enamel. The family covers over 100 languages across Basic Latin and Extended Latin in 237 glyphs.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Manual Pozor Round as a digitisation of lettering from old Slovak warning signs, where 'pozor' means warning in Slovak. The rounded terminals and filled joints replicate the effect of small details being smoothed off during enamel production, as if the letters were freshly taken from the furnace. It is the oblique version of Manual Grotesk B, with a slightly larger arc radius, and was never used alongside the upright style on a single sign.

name: Manual Pozor
classification_notes: Caps-only, no-contrast geometric sans with narrow proportions drawn from Slovak warning sign lettering. The oblique stance and rounded details place it firmly in the utilitarian geometric tradition.
summary: A digitisation of the oblique letterforms found on old Slovak warning signs, drawn by Ondrej Jób and released as a caps-only geometric sans in seven weights.
description: Derived from the stencil-drawn lettering of Slovak enamel warning signs, this typeface is the oblique counterpart to Manual Grotesk B, sharing its geometry but carrying slightly larger arc radii. It is caps-only with no stroke contrast, a narrow set-width and seven styles from Thin to Ultra, making it suited to display and headline work. Sharp corners and fine details that would have been lost in enamel production are preserved here, faithful to the original sign designers' drawings.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Manual Pozor (Slovak for 'warning') as a digitisation of the typeface from old Slovak warning signs, noting it is an oblique version of Manual Grotesk B with a slightly larger radius on its arcs. The upright and oblique styles were never combined on a single sign: Manual Grotesk marked streets and buildings while the oblique variant appeared exclusively on warning signs. The typeface preserves the sharp corners and small details that enamel production would have rounded off, presenting them as they were drawn on the original stencils.

name: Manual Grotesk B
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans-serif with no contrast, narrow width and a large x-height, based on a mid-20th century manually constructed letterform model.
summary: A digitisation of Richard Pípal's mid-20th century Slovak signage lettering, preserved and reissued by Ondrej Jób in six weights for display use.
description: Based almost without divergence on Richard Pípal's original model for a manually constructed typeface, this narrow geometric sans-serif was ubiquitous on signage across Slovak municipal buildings, administrative offices and schools during the socialist era. Six styles run from hairline to heavy, all sharing consistent no-contrast strokes and a large x-height suited to display settings. OpenType features include six stylistic sets, small capitals and ordinals, giving the family flexibility beyond its vernacular origins.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Manual Grotesk B as a utilitarian geometric sans-serif for display use, built on Richard Pípal's original manually constructed letterform model from the mid-20th century. Practically omnipresent on Slovak signage during the socialist era, it appears today on older municipal and administrative buildings. Its digitisation is framed as both a preservation effort and an attempt to reintroduce the design in new contexts. The family spans 6 styles, covers over 100 languages with 581 glyphs, and includes stylistic sets, small capitals and ordinals.

name: Manual Tatry
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a utilitarian geometric sans-serif with no contrast, narrow width, soft terminals and large x-height, digitised from hand-painted signage.
summary: A digitisation of the yellow hand-painted guideposts found in High Tatras National Park, Slovakia, rendered as a narrow geometric sans with soft terminals.
description: Ondrej Jób drew this single-weight geometric sans directly from the utilitarian hand-painted waymarkers of High Tatras National Park. Although the original signage was almost entirely uppercase, the digital version adds a full lowercase alongside special glyphs, symbols and 390 total glyphs covering over 100 languages. Stylistic set 1, discretionary ligatures and case-sensitive forms expand its usefulness for display and headline settings.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Manual Tatry as a utilitarian geometric sans-serif with soft terminals, no contrast, narrow width and a large x-height, intended primarily for display use. It is a digitisation of the yellow hand-painted guideposts in High Tatras National Park, Slovakia, completed with a full lowercase and extended Latin coverage across 390 glyphs.

name: Manual Korytnica
classification_notes: A caps-only handpainted digitisation with no contrast and narrow width, intended for display use; its organic, utilitarian character and hand-lettered origins place it firmly in the display category rather than a conventional script or sans-serif.
summary: A digitisation of hand-painted lettering from guideposts at an abandoned spa in Korytnica, Slovakia, released by Ondrej Jób as a caps-only, narrow display typeface.
description: Each glyph exists in two variants that rotate automatically to reproduce the irregular rhythm of hand-painted lettering. The design shares construction principles with Manual Grotesk but carries its own character, shaped by whoever originally painted the spa's signage. With 386 glyphs covering over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin, it includes proportional lining figures, standard ligatures and ordinals.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Manual Korytnica as a digitisation of the hand-painted type found on green guideposts at an abandoned spa in Korytnica, Slovakia. Caps-only, organic and utilitarian with soft terminals and no contrast, it is built for display use in a single style. Every glyph ships in two variants that rotate automatically to mimic the hand-painted effect, and the family covers over 100 languages with 386 glyphs in total.

name: Woodkit Solid
classification_notes: A caps-only, experimental display typeface built on a strict modular grid where every glyph occupies a perfect square, inspired by wooden type blocks and letterpress printing. Its decorative, constructed nature places it firmly in the display category.
summary: A caps-only modular display typeface by Ondrej Jób in which every glyph fills a perfect square, referencing the physicality of wooden printing blocks.
description: Part of a three-family type system inspired by wood type and letterpress, Woodkit Solid comprises six distinct styles covering alphabet designs, components and ornaments. The defining structural rule — each glyph occupies an equal square both horizontally and vertically — is a direct homage to physical wooden blocks, and yields surprising interchangeability between styles and a playful, building-block quality. Across 1,150 glyphs it covers Basic Latin, Extended Latin, Cyrillic and Greek, with tabular and proportional figure sets and contextual alternates for additional versatility.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Woodkit Solid as a caps-only, decorative and experimental constructed typeface intended for display use. Its most important feature, for both aesthetic and functional reasons, is that every glyph fills a perfect square — a reference to real wooden blocks — which unlocks interchangeability between styles, unexpected constructional solutions and a playfulness akin to building with blocks. It forms part of the broader Woodkit system alongside Print and Reprint.

name: Pexico
classification_notes: Pexico is a pixel-constructed typeface built on a 10×14 grid with mixed terminals, including a monospaced style among its eight variants; it sits closest to mono/pixel as its defining structural characteristic.
summary: A pixel-constructed typeface on a 10×14 grid, offering eight stylistic variants from text-ready Regular and Mono to display-focused Dots, Round, Outline and Inverse.
description: Built on a strict 10×14 pixel grid, Pexico aligns precisely with a display's pixel grid at 20 pt or its multiples, making it suited to screen-first work. Its eight styles split into four text settings — Regular, Bold, Narrow and Mono — and four display variations of the Regular, including Outline, Dots, Round and Inverse. Coverage extends across Basic Latin, Extended Latin, Cyrillic and Greek for over 109 languages, supported by 920 glyphs and a full suite of figure styles and OpenType features.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Pexico as a pixel-constructed typeface with mixed terminals intended for versatile use, built on a 10×14 grid in eight styles. Four styles — Regular, Bold, Narrow and Mono — serve text setting, while Outline, Dots, Round and Inverse offer stylistic display variations. The family supports Basic Latin, Extended Latin, Cyrillic and Greek across 109 languages with 920 glyphs and features including small capitals, stylistic sets, discretionary ligatures and multiple figure styles.

name: Woodkit Reprint
classification_notes: A caps-only, experimental constructed typeface where every glyph fills a perfect square, inspired by physical wood type and letterpress printing. Unambiguously display in intent and form.
summary: A caps-only constructed display family from Ondrej Jób where every glyph fills a perfect square, drawing directly from wood type and letterpress tradition.
description: Part of the wider Woodkit system alongside Solid and Print, Reprint offers six distinct styles ranging from outline letterpress forms to solid blocks and ornamental characters. The square constraint on every glyph is both a structural rule and a conceptual nod to the physical wooden block, making characters interchangeable and visually consistent across combinations. With 1,150 glyphs across Latin, Cyrillic and Greek, it covers over 109 languages and includes tabular lining, proportional lining and oldstyle figures alongside standard and discretionary ligatures.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Woodkit as a type system inspired by wood type and letterpress, consisting of three families — Solid, Print and Reprint — each with six distinct styles. The defining rule is that every glyph fills a square both horizontally and vertically, referencing the physicality of real wooden blocks. This constraint opened up new constructional solutions, mutual interchangeability between styles and a sense of playfulness, with some forms directly inspired by wooden alphabet blocks for children.

name: Woodkit Print
classification_notes: Caps-only, modular, experimental typeface inspired by wood type and letterpress tradition, with every glyph filling a strict square grid — firmly in the display/decorative category.
summary: A caps-only, modular display family by Ondrej Jób built on a strict square grid as an homage to physical wooden type blocks.
description: Part of the wider Woodkit type system alongside Solid and Reprint, Print comprises six styles rooted in the aesthetic of wood type and children's alphabet blocks. Every glyph fills its square not just horizontally but vertically, a constraint that drives unexpected constructional solutions and enforces visual interchangeability between styles. The family covers over 109 languages across Basic Latin, Extended Latin, Cyrillic and Greek, with tabular lining, proportional lining and proportional oldstyle figures alongside ligatures and contextual alternates.
foundry_blurb: Woodkit Print is described by Setup Type as a caps-only, decorative, experimental constructed typeface for display use, forming one part of the three-family Woodkit system inspired by wood type and letterpress. Its defining feature is that every glyph fills a perfect square — both horizontally and vertically — referencing the physicality of real wooden blocks, enabling interchangeability between styles and a quality of playfulness the foundry likens to building with blocks.

name: Remi
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a monospaced sans-serif with no stroke contrast; proportions derived from classic segmented LCD displays, placing it firmly in the geometric monospace category.
summary: A geometric monospaced sans-serif by Ondrej Jób, its proportions and construction drawn from classic segmented LCD displays across ten styles from Light to Black.
description: Remi has no stroke contrast, a natural width and a notably small x-height, with capitals sharing the same height as the ascender and the x-height sitting at exactly half the cap height. A secondary set of shorter capitals is included, accessible as small capitals or via Stylistic Set 1. The 720-glyph character set covers over 100 languages and includes tabular lining and oldstyle figures, fractions, small capitals, contextual alternates and more than 40 special ligatures for metric and imperial measurement units.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Remi as an experimental, industrial monospaced sans-serif intended for versatile use, with construction and proportions based on classic segmented LCD fonts. The family spans 10 styles from Light to Black with italics, supports Basic Latin and Extended Latin across 100-plus languages, and includes 720 glyphs with a broad suite of OpenType features including five stylistic sets, small capitals and discretionary ligatures.

name: Odesta
classification_notes: Odesta is explicitly described as a decorative script display typeface with detached strokes and ball terminals, placing it firmly in the script category with a display-oriented, ornamental subcategory.
summary: An ornamental display script by Ondrej Jób with high contrast, detached strokes and ball terminals, spanning seven weights from Hair to Black.
description: Released in 2013, Odesta is a narrow, high-contrast script typeface built around detached strokes and ball terminals, designed specifically for display settings. Seven weights from Hair to Black allow a wide tonal range while the medium x-height and ornamental character keep it firmly in headline territory. Its 983-glyph set includes small capitals, swashes, initial and final forms, discretionary ligatures and multiple figure styles, with coverage of over 100 Latin languages.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Odesta as an elegant, ornamental script typeface with ball terminals, primarily intended for display use. It comprises 7 styles from Hair to Black with very high contrast, narrow width and medium x-height, supporting Basic and Extended Latin across more than 100 languages, with a total of 983 glyphs and a broad range of OpenType features including swashes, small capitals, titling alternates and multiple figure sets.

name: Pexico Micro
classification_notes: A pixel-constructed typeface built on a 5×7 grid with a Mono style variant, making it firmly monospaced in character even across its display variants. Its grid-locked construction and screen-alignment precision place it squarely in the pixel/bitmap tradition.
summary: A pixel-constructed typeface by Ondrej Jób built on a 5×7 grid, offered in eight styles spanning text and display use.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób and released in 2014, it uses a strict 5×7 pixel grid that aligns to display pixel grids at 10 pt and its multiples. The eight styles divide into four text-oriented cuts — Regular, Bold, Narrow and Mono — and four display variants — Outline, Dots, Round and Inverse. Despite its grid constraints, the family carries over 922 glyphs with coverage across Latin, Extended Latin, Cyrillic and Greek, including oldstyle figures, small capitals and fractions.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Pexico Micro as a pixel-constructed typeface with mixed terminals built for versatile use. It features eight styles, a 5×7 capital grid, support for over 109 languages across Basic Latin, Extended Latin, Cyrillic and Greek, and a comprehensive set of OpenType features including small capitals, discretionary ligatures and multiple figure styles.

name: Doko Sans
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a humanist sans-serif with angled terminals and brush origins; low contrast and natural proportions align with the humanist sans tradition.
summary: A 14-style humanist sans with brush-derived angled terminals, covering Latin, Cyrillic, Greek and Vietnamese across 1,427 glyphs.
description: Doko Sans is the sans-serif companion to Doko, retaining the parent family's brush origins through angled terminals and an expressive character. The family runs from Hair to Black with matching italics, giving 14 styles with low contrast, natural width and medium x-height. Language support spans over 110 languages, with a full suite of figure styles and OpenType features including small caps, swashes and four stylistic sets.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Doko Sans as an elegant, expressive humanist sans-serif with angled terminals intended for versatile use. The family consists of 14 styles from Hair to Black including italics, with low contrast, natural width and medium x-height. It supports Basic Latin, Extended Latin, Vietnamese, Cyrillic and Greek across 110-plus languages, with 1,427 glyphs including multiple figure styles, small capitals, swashes, discretionary ligatures and ordinals.

name: Bismuth
classification_notes: Caps-only, no-contrast, industrially constructed letterforms with angular, sci-fi character explicitly intended for display use place this firmly in the display category with a geometric, constructed subcategory.
summary: A caps-only, no-contrast display typeface with angular, industrial geometry across nine weights plus a free Symbols companion font.
description: Constructed with no stroke contrast and a consistent wide width across all nine weights from Hair to Ultra, Bismuth reads as a geometric, sci-fi display face where upper and lower case pairs share identical widths but differ in construction detail. Both cases are capitalised in form, making weights freely interchangeable without reflow. Coverage extends to over 100 languages via Basic and Extended Latin, and the accompanying Symbols font adds more than 100 arrows, patterns and miscellaneous marks at no additional cost.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Bismuth as an expressive angular display typeface with sci-fi character, caps-only and experimentally constructed, available in ten styles from Hair to Black with no contrast and wide width. It covers over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin with 380 glyphs, and ships with a free Symbols font containing over 100 arrows, symbols and patterns.

name: Bismuth Stencil
classification_notes: A caps-only geometric stencil typeface with no stroke contrast and consistent width across weights, designed explicitly for display use with an industrial and sci-fi character.
summary: A caps-only geometric stencil display typeface by Ondrej Jób, spanning nine weights from Hair to Ultra with consistent width across all styles.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób and released in 2012, Bismuth Stencil is an angular, constructed typeface with no stroke contrast and a fixed width that holds steady across all nine weights. Both uppercase and lowercase slots are capitals, differing in construction but sharing identical widths, which makes weights freely interchangeable without reflow. A companion Symbols font adds over 100 arrows, patterns and miscellaneous marks, and the family covers more than 100 languages across Basic Latin and Extended Latin.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Bismuth Stencil as an expressive angular stencil display typeface with sci-fi character, available in ten styles from Hair to Black. It is caps-only with no contrast and wide width, supporting Basic Latin and Extended Latin across more than 100 languages with 380 glyphs. A free companion Symbols font containing over 100 arrows, symbols and patterns accompanies the nine paid styles.

name: Doko
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes Doko as a humanist serif with low contrast and calligraphic influences drawn from cartoons, illustration and lettering, placing it firmly in the humanist serif subcategory.
summary: A humanist serif by Ondrej Jób with calligraphic roots in cartoons and lettering, designed to function across editorial text and display contexts in four styles.
description: Originating as a final project at the Type and Media masters programme at KABK The Hague, Doko balances expressive display qualities with the legibility demands of text setting. Its low contrast, natural width and medium x-height give it warmth without sacrificing functionality, while features including four figure sets, discretionary ligatures and three stylistic sets allow for detailed typographic control. Coverage extends to over 100 languages across Basic Latin and Extended Latin in 479 glyphs.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Doko as an elegant, expressive humanist serif intended for versatile use, with four styles from Regular to Bold with italics. Inspired by cartoons, illustration and lettering, it sits between expressive display typefaces and functional text faces, performing well in both contexts. It supports over 100 languages and includes a full range of figure styles and OpenType features.

name: ION A
classification_notes: ION A is explicitly LCD-inspired, built on a 7-segment display matrix with no stroke contrast and a compressed, modular construction. Its segmented, geometric structure places it firmly in the display/experimental category rather than any conventional sans or serif classification.
summary: A modular, LCD-inspired display typeface by Ondrej Jób, built on a 7-segment display matrix in 11 styles from Thin to an ultra-heavy U3.
description: Constructed on the grid of a classic 7-segment LCD display, ION A has no stroke contrast, a compressed width and a large x-height across ten weights plus a special Cells style containing filled and outlined containers for layered compositions. The family covers over 100 languages via Basic Latin and Extended Latin, with 435 glyphs including tabular lining figures, superscripts, subscripts and a slashed zero. It is the condensed member of the ION collection, sitting alongside the normal-width ION B and wide ION C.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe ION A as an experimental, modular constructed typeface intended primarily for display use, based on the matrix of the classic 7-segment LCD display. It ships in 11 styles from Thin to Black, with no contrast, a compressed width and a large x-height, supports over 100 languages, and includes a special Cells style for building layered or standalone compositions.

name: Clip
classification_notes: Clip is classified as display due to its single-stroke construction and decorative nature; it draws on traditional serif proportions and contrast but is conceptually experimental, making it unsuitable for running text.
summary: A single-stroke display serif by Ondrej Jób in which every glyph is drawn as one uninterrupted line, inspired by the form of a paperclip.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób and released in 2011, Clip borrows its proportions, contrast and construction from traditional serif text typefaces while imposing a strict constraint: each glyph is formed from a single, continuous, unbroken stroke. Three weights run from Hair to Regular, all at natural width with a large x-height. The 749-glyph set covers over 100 languages across Basic and Extended Latin, with proportional oldstyle and lining figures, four stylistic sets, ligatures, swashes and ordinals for typographic flexibility.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Clip as a conceptual, experimental modern serif typeface with rounded terminals, primarily intended for display use. Inspired by the shape of a paperclip, it draws its construction, proportions and contrast from traditional serif text typefaces, with the defining characteristic that every glyph is drawn in a single uninterrupted stroke. Three styles are available, supporting over 100 languages with a total of 749 glyphs.

name: Klimax
classification_notes: Klimax is classified as a display typeface by the foundry, with extreme weight contrast between Plus and Minus optical variants and wide proportions; its humanist sans construction and true cursive italics place it firmly in display-oriented humanist territory.
summary: A conceptual display sans by Ondrej Jób that pairs an ultra-heavy Plus with a hairline Minus, both sharing identical metrics so they can swap within a single line of text.
description: Klimax explores extreme weight opposition through two optical variants, Plus and Minus, where the counters of Plus precisely echo the stroke width of Minus. Four styles including true cursive italics make up the family, with italic strokes finished in curved protruding terminals that lend a calligraphic softness. Wide proportions and a large x-height suit it to headline and editorial work where contrast is the primary instrument.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe Klimax as a conceptual, experimental humanist sans-serif primarily for display use. The four-style family features high contrast, wide width and a large x-height, with Plus and Minus styles sharing metrics so they can be interchanged without altering text length. It covers over 100 languages across Basic Latin and Extended Latin, with 1176 glyphs and OpenType features including ligatures, small capitals and case-sensitive forms.

name: ION B
classification_notes: ION B is explicitly modular and constructed, based on the 7-segment LCD display matrix. Its geometric, no-contrast, narrow construction places it firmly in the display/experimental category rather than any conventional sans-serif classification.
summary: A modular display typeface built on the 7-segment LCD display matrix, available in 11 styles from Thin to an ultra-heavy Cells variant.
description: Constructed from the grid of a classic 7-segment LCD display, ION B offers ten weights from Thin to Black alongside a special Cells style containing containers and boxes for compositional use. The family sits within the ION collection alongside condensed ION A and wide ION C, each sharing the same construction logic. With no stroke contrast and a large x-height, it covers over 100 languages through Basic and Extended Latin, with OpenType features including stylistic sets, discretionary ligatures and fractions.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe ION B as an experimental, modular constructed typeface for display use, comprising 11 styles from Thin to Black with no contrast, narrow width and a large x-height. Based on the classic 7-segment LCD display matrix, it includes a special Cells style for creating layered or standalone compositions. It supports over 100 languages via Basic and Extended Latin and includes tabular lining figures, superscript and subscript numerals, and features such as stylistic sets, fractions and ordinals.

name: ION C
classification_notes: ION C is constructed on the matrix of the classic 7-segment LCD display, making it a modular display typeface with no contrast and a monospaced, geometric construction derived from digital segmented readouts.
summary: A modular display typeface built on the 7-segment LCD grid, released in 11 styles from Thin to a trio of ultra-heavy weights, with a companion Cells style for building grid-based compositions.
description: Designed by Ondrej Jób and first released in 2010, ION C is the wide variant in the ION collection alongside the condensed ION A and normal-width ION B. Its construction follows the matrix of the classic 7-segment LCD display, producing a uniform stroke with no contrast and a large x-height across ten weights. An eleventh style, Cells, provides filled and outlined containers with varying corner radii for layering beneath text or constructing standalone layouts. Over 100 languages are supported through Basic and Extended Latin, while the three heaviest ultra weights carry only the basic glyph set due to their simplified construction.
foundry_blurb: Setup Type describe ION C as an experimental, modular constructed typeface primarily intended for display use, consisting of 11 styles from Thin to Black with no contrast, natural width and a large x-height. Based on the classic 7-segment LCD display matrix, it supports over 100 languages and includes features such as stylistic sets, discretionary ligatures, fractions, subscript and superscript figures, and ordinals. The family forms part of the wider ION collection alongside the condensed ION A and normal-width ION B.
